topic.title: Setting Up the Test Scenario on the ML Console

body.p:
The code fragments from the previous chapter are prepared below for an ML console session:
UWE test footnote (40/40, callout N): DITA fn maps to UWE footnote/key/desc.
UWE supplement (fn-add) for the footnote index.

li.p: Creating the temporal properties: validStart, validEnd, systemStart, systemEnd
Creating the indexes for searching over time ranges: database-range-field-index("dateTime", "systemStart",...
Creating the two time axes system and valid Temporal Time Axes

the individual steps are called as functions via xdmp:invoke-function. This construct is normally used to declare a function anonymously anonymous function and call it as a transactionTransactions. MarkLogic offers further options MarkLogic transactions for working transactionally.
To simulate the 5 days between the loss report and the recovery, an xdmp:sleep statement was inserted between the creation of the documents.

body.p: If we run this query on a fresh database, we get the following result:

fig.desc: After executing the above query, the DB contains three documents: the original, the split document, and the update. The split document and the original are marked as predecessors with a suffix.
